Fever & Pain Relief.name: Paracetamol (Acetaminophen)
Fever & Pain Relief.usage: Used for mild to moderate fever or pain relief.
Fever & Pain Relief.dosage: Usually 500–1000 mg every 6 hours after meals if needed.
Fever & Pain Relief.notes: Avoid exceeding 4 grams per day. Use with caution in liver disease.

Fever & Pain Relief.name: Ibuprofen
Fever & Pain Relief.usage: Relieves headache, muscle pain, toothache, menstrual cramps, and fever.
Fever & Pain Relief.notes: Avoid on an empty stomach. Not recommended in stomach ulcers or kidney disease.

Fever & Pain Relief.name: Aspirin (Low Dose)
Fever & Pain Relief.usage: Used occasionally for pain or fever.
Fever & Pain Relief.notes: Avoid in children and people with ulcers or bleeding risk.

Cold, Cough & Allergy.name: Cetirizine
Cold, Cough & Allergy.usage: Relieves sneezing, runny nose, and allergies.
Cold, Cough & Allergy.dosage: 5–10 mg once daily, preferably at night.
Cold, Cough & Allergy.notes: May cause mild drowsiness in some people.

Cold, Cough & Allergy.name: Loratadine
Cold, Cough & Allergy.usage: Used for seasonal allergies and hay fever.

Cold, Cough & Allergy.name: Diphenhydramine
Cold, Cough & Allergy.usage: Used for dry cough or allergy-related symptoms.
Cold, Cough & Allergy.notes: Can cause drowsiness. Avoid driving after taking.

Cold, Cough & Allergy.name: Dextromethorphan
Cold, Cough & Allergy.notes: Do not use with MAOIs or in persistent productive cough.

Cold, Cough & Allergy.name: Phenylephrine
Cold, Cough & Allergy.notes: Avoid in high blood pressure or heart disease.

Digestive Issues.name: Oral Rehydration Salts (ORS)
Digestive Issues.usage: Prevents dehydration from diarrhea or heat.
Digestive Issues.dosage: Mix in clean water; sip frequently.
Digestive Issues.notes: Safe for most people, including children.

Digestive Issues.name: Domperidone
Digestive Issues.dosage: 10 mg before meals, up to 3 times daily.
Digestive Issues.notes: Avoid long-term use or in heart rhythm disorders.

Digestive Issues.name: Simethicone
Digestive Issues.dosage: 40–125 mg after meals and at bedtime.

Digestive Issues.name: Loperamide
Digestive Issues.dosage: 2 mg after each loose stool (max 8 mg/day).
Digestive Issues.notes: Avoid if high fever or bloody stools present.

Digestive Issues.name: Pantoprazole
Digestive Issues.notes: Consult a doctor if symptoms persist for more than 2 weeks.

Skin & Minor Injuries.name: Povidone Iodine Ointment
Skin & Minor Injuries.usage: Used to disinfect minor cuts and wounds.
Skin & Minor Injuries.dosage: Apply gently after cleaning the area.
Skin & Minor Injuries.notes: Avoid using on deep wounds without supervision.

Skin & Minor Injuries.name: Hydrocortisone Cream (1%)
Skin & Minor Injuries.usage: Used for mild skin irritation, itching, or rashes.
Skin & Minor Injuries.dosage: Apply thinly twice daily for up to 7 days.
Skin & Minor Injuries.notes: Do not apply on broken or infected skin.

Cold & Flu Combo Packs.name: Sinarest Tablet
Cold & Flu Combo Packs.usage: Relieves headache, nasal congestion, and cold symptoms.
Cold & Flu Combo Packs.dosage: One tablet every 8 hours after meals.
Cold & Flu Combo Packs.notes: Avoid driving if drowsy. Not for children below 12 years.

Cold & Flu Combo Packs.name: D-Cold Total
Cold & Flu Combo Packs.usage: Used for cold, fever, and nasal congestion.
Cold & Flu Combo Packs.dosage: One tablet every 8 hours after food.
Cold & Flu Combo Packs.notes: Avoid if hypertensive or with thyroid problems.

General Supplements.name: Vitamin C (Ascorbic Acid)
General Supplements.usage: Boosts immunity and helps in recovery from cold.

General Supplements.name: Multivitamin Tablets
General Supplements.usage: Supports general health and energy levels.
General Supplements.notes: Avoid overdosing; not a substitute for balanced diet.
